description:
You are acting as a junior backup-operator who has just received a freshly-restored
    backup manifest that must be sanity-checked before the data can be declared usable.
    Everything you need is already on disk; you only have to analyse some JSON, validate
    it with jq, and write two small output files that the automated test-suite will read.

    1.  Location of the material to inspect  
        • Manifest to validate: /home/user/backups/latest/manifest.json  
        • Corresponding schema: /home/user/schema/restore-schema.json  

    2.  Validation requirements  
        a.  The top-level key “backupId” must exist **and** have the exact value
            BK2023-10-05 (no quotes in the log, obviously the JSON value is a string).  
        b.  Under .restorePoints (an array) each element must contain the 4
            non-null keys: id, timestamp, sizeBytes, checksum.  
        c.  If and only if every restore point satisfies the rule in 2 b **and**
            the rule in 2 a is true, the validation is considered to have **passed**.

    3.  Required output files  
        (The tests compare files byte-for-byte, so follow the formats exactly.)  

        3.1  /home/user/restore_validation.log  
             • One single-line file, no line breaks before or after, UTF-8 text.  
             • Exact content if validation passes (including punctuation and case):  
               VALID: All 4 required fields present for 3 restore points.  
             • If you somehow detect an error you may write  
               INVALID: <brief-explanation>  
               but the expected happy-path for grading is the VALID line above.  

        3.2  /home/user/restore_summary.json  
             • Pretty-printed JSON (2-space indentation, newline after each item).  
             • Keys must appear in the following order:  
               backupId, restorePointCount, totalSizeBytes, generatedBy  
             • The “generatedBy” field must be the literal string "AI-Agent".  
             • Example of the **exact** formatting the grader will look for  
               (values shown below are the correct ones for the supplied manifest):  
               {
                 "backupId": "BK2023-10-05",
                 "restorePointCount": 3,
                 "totalSizeBytes": 157286400,
                 "generatedBy": "AI-Agent"
               }

    4.  Hints (you are free to choose any method, but keep total command count ≈5-10)  
        • `jq` can both validate field presence (`has`) and do arithmetic reductions
          (e.g. add all .sizeBytes).  
        • You do not need any external schema validators; jq alone is sufficient here.  
        • Remember to write files under /home/user/ with ordinary user permissions.  

    5.  Success criteria checked by the grader  
        • /home/user/restore_validation.log exists and contains **exactly** the VALID
          line shown in section 3.1.  
        • /home/user/restore_summary.json exists, is valid JSON, honours the key order
          and value formats given in section 3.2, and the numeric totals are correct
          for the manifest that ships with this exercise.  
        • No other files need to be modified or created.  
        • All tasks can realistically be completed in 5-10 shell commands
          (cd, jq, echo/redirection, maybe cat for inspection).
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory structure and files -->
    /home/user/backups/latest/manifest.json
    ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    {
      "backupId": "BK2023-10-05",
      "createdBy": "backupd v2.4.1",
      "restorePoints": [
        {
          "id": "rp1",
          "timestamp": "2023-10-05T01:15:00Z",
          "sizeBytes": 52428800,
          "checksum": "c1a1e3f4"
        },
        {
          "id": "rp2",
          "timestamp": "2023-10-05T03:30:00Z",
          "sizeBytes": 52428800,
          "checksum": "d2b2e4f5"
        },
        {
          "id": "rp3",
          "timestamp": "2023-10-05T06:45:00Z",
          "sizeBytes": 52428800,
          "checksum": "e3c3f5a6"
        }
      ]
    }

    /home/user/schema/restore-schema.json
    ─────────────────────────────────────
    {
      "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["backupId", "restorePoints"],
      "properties": {
        "backupId": { "type": "string", "const": "BK2023-10-05" },
        "restorePoints": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "object",
            "required": ["id", "timestamp", "sizeBytes", "checksum"],
            "properties": {
              "id":        { "type": "string" },
              "timestamp": { "type": "string", "format": "date-time" },
              "sizeBytes": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 0 },
              "checksum":  { "type": "string" }
            }
          },
          "minItems": 1
        }
      }
    }

    <!-- Expected artefacts AFTER the agent completes the task -->
    /home/user/restore_validation.log
    ────────────────────────────────
    VALID: All 4 required fields present for 3 restore points.

    /home/user/restore_summary.json
    ───────────────────────────────
    {
      "backupId": "BK2023-10-05",
      "restorePointCount": 3,
      "totalSizeBytes": 157286400,
      "generatedBy": "AI-Agent"
    }
